data.title: Unit 1B: Clothes - Lesson 1: What are you wearing?
data.desc:
Tech Test- Clothes: T-shirt, dress, skirt, sweater, jacket
- What are you wearing? - I'm wearing a T-shirt.

data.title: Unit 1B: Clothes - Lesson 2: What are they wearing?
data.desc:
- Review words in L1
 - clothes (cont): pants, jeans, shorts, sandals, shoes
Review: 
- What are you wearing? - I'm wearing a T-shirt and jeans.
New: 
 - What are they wearing? - They are wearing T-sh

data.title: Unit 1B: Clothes - Lesson 3: What is he wearing?
data.desc:
- Review words in L2
 - clothes (cont): scarf, tie, belt
- What is he/she wearing? - He/She is wearing a shirt and a belt.
- Is she wearing sandals? Yes, she is./ No, she isn't. She's wearing sneakers

data.title: Unit 1B: Clothes - Lesson 4: They're wearing T-shirts and jeans.
data.desc:
- long vowel /ɜː/: shirt, skirt, girl (eg: a girl in a shirt and a skirt.)
- hat, cap, socks
- What are you wearing? - I'm wearing a shirt and a cap.
 - What is he/she wearing?

data.title: Unit 1B: Clothes - Lesson 5: I'm wearing a T-shirt.
data.desc:
- Review
- Value: School uniforms
 - Are they wearing ties? Yes, they are./ No, they aren't. They're wearing belts.
 - Is she wearing a belt? Yes, she is./ No, she isn't. She's wearing a tie.

data.title: Unit 2B: Sports - Lesson 1: What can you do?
data.desc:
- Sport and exercise: run, ride a bike, swim, skip, skateboard
- What can you do? I can swim.
- Can you swim? Yes, I can./ No, I can't.

data.title: Unit 2B: Sports - Lesson 2: I can play football but I can't play basketball.
data.desc:
- Review words in L1
- Sports and exercise (con.t): play football, play basketball, play volleyball, play badminton, play tennis
- What can you do? I can swim and skip.
- Can you play football? Yes, I

data.title: Unit 2B: Sports - Lesson 3: What can he do?
data.desc:
- Review words in L2
- Sports and exercise (con.t): play tennis, play chess, play golf
- What can he do? He can play chess.
- What can she do? She can play chess.

data.title: Unit 2B: Sports - Lesson 4: She can swim and do karate.
data.desc:
- consonant: /ʃ/ (chef, fish, dish, eg: the chef makes a dish of fish.)
- Review words in L3
- do karate, do yoga
- What can he do? He can swim and do karate.
- What can she do? She can swim and do ka

data.title: Unit 2B: Sports - Lesson 5: He can swim but he can't do karate.
data.desc:
- Review all
- Health: Be active. Exercise every day.
'- What can you do? I can swim and skip.
- Can you play football? Yes, I can./ No, I can't.
- I can play football but I can't play basketball.

data.title: Unit 3B: Food - Lesson 1: Do you like grapes?
data.desc:
- Fruit: peach, coconut, grapes
- Vegetables: corn, mushroom
- Do you like grapes? Yes, I do./ No, I don't.  I like coconuts.
- Do you like corns? Yes, I do./ No, I don't.  I like mushrooms.

data.title: Unit 3B: Food - Lesson 2: I like pineapples and pears.
data.desc:
- Review words in L1
- Fruit: pear, pineapple
- Vegetables: cabbage, broccoli
- Do you like cucumbers? Yes, I do./ No, I don't.

data.title: Unit 3B: Food - Lesson 3: I like cucumbers but I don't like pumpkins.
data.desc:
- Review words in L2
- Fruit: pomelo, starfruit
- Vegetables: cucumber, pumpkin
- I like pomelos and strawberries.
- I like starfruits but I don't like pomelos.

data.title: Unit 3B: Food - Lesson 4: Do they like onions?
data.desc:
consonant: /tʃ/ (teacher, cheese, peach: Teacher Richard likes peaches and cheese.)
- Review words in L3 + some others from L1+L2
- Fruit: jackfruit, strawberry
- Vegetables: onion
- Does he like onio

data.title: Unit 3B: Food - Lesson 5: Do they like onions?
data.desc:
- Review the rest of the vocab
- Healthy lifestyle: Eat more fruit and vegetables!
- Do you like cucumbers? Yes, I do./ No, I don't. I like pumpkins.
- I like pineapples and pears.
- I like cucumbers
